posts.caption:
The Odyssey is named after a man, but read it again and you’ll find the women are the ones who actually run the show.
I’ve joined @readingsbooks Big Book Club working through Homer’s Odyssey. Five sessions with academics and authors, timed to Christopher Nolan’s adaptation landing in July. This is what I learned from Session 1 with Dr Seth Robinson.
Athena engineers the entire return. Circe gives Odysseus the knowledge to navigate the underworld. Nausicaa saves him on the beach. Ino saves him from drowning. Even Calypso hands him the tools to leave. The women are the ones who hold all the knowledge and the so-called monsters he defeats with it, Scylla, Charybdis, the Sirens, were only ever defending their own space.
And then there’s Penelope. Weaving by day, unpicking it by night, buying herself time with the one form of power she’s allowed…the loom and the sacred space it wields for her.
But the part that has stayed with me most was the translation. Robinson showed two versions of the same passage from Book 22, the killing of the maids. One translator calls them women, but Emily Wilson calls them girls. One frames it as deserved; the other shows you the pain. Translation is never an unbiased, neutral act. It will always tell you something about the person doing it, and the time they’re living in.
So here’s the question I’m sitting with: if this is the foundational text of the Western world, what does it say about us now?
Full essay on Substack 🔗 link in bio. Session 2 goes deeper into the women: Penelope, Helen, Calypso, Circe, Nausicaa.
What did you think of The Odyssey, if you’ve read it? Tell me below 👇🏼
In partnership with @readingsbooks

posts.caption: About textile crafts - magical power - women: When you have a look at the old fairytales of Germany, France and Northern Europe, these stories are also filled with that pattern. In the "Nibelungenlied" (the story of Sigfried the Dragonslayer, who gets to bath in dragonsblood which makes him uninjurable besides a very small spot on his shoulder and later gets killed through that spot by Hagen von Tronje) Krimhild, Sigfrieds Wife wants to protect him and markes the spot on his tunic with red thread. Also in the beginning of the story she and her women (she is a princess, so this is her household staff) are described to make lots of clothes for the men, also to embroider them. They did it for protection, also for presenting their social status.

posts.alt_text: After the conclusion of HBO's buzzy coming-of-age drama, "Euphoria," @HunterSchafer is ready for her Saturn return. As the freshly minted face of @MuglerOfficial's Angel Blush Eau de Parfum, a new take on a beloved fragrance rolling out nationwide this fall, Schafer shares that working with the brand is a dream. “I was watching those hourlong runway shows that you can find on YouTube when I was in high school,” she says, adding that Mugler was always an “enormous sort of vault of inspiration” for her. At the link in bio, the actor reflects with Vogue on the end of "Euphoria," and how she's preparing for her busiest year yet. Photos: @carlijnjacobs

posts.alt_text:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.

She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”

Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.

For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile. 

Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.

posts.caption:
Are you a true autumn, a soft summer, or even a bright spring? Color analysis is enjoying a renaissance among young shoppers obsessed with personal style and aesthetic auras. For @CurateYourStyle, which aims to use such fashion formulas to curb overconsumption, it’s about encouraging shoppers to buy fewer, better clothes. But can it live up to its promises?
Sustainability editor @BellaWebb_ headed to @MaisonColbert, a home designed by artists @ColbertCharlotte and @PhilipColbert, to meet Curate Your Style co-founders Hannah and Sophia Rashad and put color analysis to the test. Read how the London-based personal styling platform is tapping into color formulas to help shoppers cut through the constant onslaught of fashion advertising at the link in our bio.
Video and edit: @ItsNiall_ @_AureliaRachel

posts.caption: After a two-year slump, global luxury spending will continue to grow in 2026, forecasted between 0-2% on a constant currency basis, per a new report from consultancy firm @BainandCompany. Despite macroeconomic uncertainty and global unrest, the luxury industry shows positive signals for the near and long-term future. @josephdbobowicz unpacks the key findings at the link in our bio.
posts.alt_text: Today, London-born label @StudioNicholson unveils its first runway collection after 16 years in business and an impressive growth trajectory. The stakes are high, but the brains behind the business are confident that a runway show is a logical next step that could reap a major return on investment. At the link in our bio, @josephdbobowicz speaks to founder and creative director @NickWakeman about the brand’s ongoing strategy devised to build authority around the globe.

Indian track and field athlete and Vogue Values 2026 honouree Jyothi Yarraji (@jyothi_yarraji_) clocked 12.99s to strike gold in the women’s 100m hurdles at the 65th National Inter-State Senior Athletics Championships. Earlier in the day, she clocked 13.14s in the heats to achieve the qualifying standard for the 20th Asian Games Aichi–Nagoya 2026.

posts.caption:
Since launching his label in 2017, Abhinav Mishra (@abhinavmishra_) has made mirror work his calling card, giving the traditional craft a fresh identity. His latest bridal collection, Baradari, builds on that signature while embracing a more glamorous Indo-Western mood. Think voluminous lehengas, sharply tailored blouses, regal sherwanis and the label’s much-loved kurtas, all inspired by aristocratic style with subtle Mongolian influences.
Discover the collection at Vogue Wedding Atelier 2026, curated as India’s most luxurious showcase of bridal couture and high jewellery. Request an invite at the link in bio.
#VogueWeddingAtelier #VWA2026

posts.caption:
Chaos is something @GracieAbrams has grown accustomed to. Eight years ago, she was a first-year student at Barnard with a cult fandom she’d fostered through Instagram; soon she was signed to Interscope, then on her first tour, then opening for Olivia Rodrigo and Taylor Swift. By the end of 2024, Abrams was a Grammy-nominated pop star filling arenas.
She finally took a breather last summer. “It’s not until you stop that you realize ‘Oh, I haven’t seen my friends in a long time. I haven’t cooked a meal in a long time,’” Abrams says. “The sleep schedule becomes regular for the first time in, like, four years.”
Now 26, she’s shifting into a new gear. Abrams’s third studio album, “Daughter from Hell,” out in July, sounds like her—earnest, introspective, folk-informed—but the production is more lush (“It feels like me in progress,” she says); she’s also in a happy relationship and making her first film, “Please,” with the filmmaker Halina Reijn.
For Vogue’s Summer 2026 issue, Brittany Spanos meets Gracie Abrams in Los Angeles and New York to talk about growing up, slowing down, falling in love, and where she’d like to go next. Tap the link in our bio to read the full profile.
Photographed by @larissahofmann, Written by @brittanyspanos, Styled by @jordenbickham, Hair by @tamastuzes, Makeup by @emikaneko, Manicure by @1.800.nailme, Tailoring by Irina Tshartaryan for Susie’s Custom Designs, Produced by @hyperion.la, Set Design by  @patienceharding. Shot on location @lucasmuseum. Vogue, Summer 2026.
posts.alt_text: It’s always fashion week somewhere, but the World Cup waits for no one—not even @Skepta. Tap the link in our bio for the best celebrity sightings from men’s fashion week so far. Video by @styledumonde

posts.caption: After the conclusion of HBO's buzzy coming-of-age drama, "Euphoria," @HunterSchafer is ready for her Saturn return. As the freshly minted face of @MuglerOfficial's Angel Blush Eau de Parfum, a new take on a beloved fragrance rolling out nationwide this fall, Schafer shares that working with the brand is a dream. “I was watching those hourlong runway shows that you can find on YouTube when I was in high school,” she says, adding that Mugler was always an “enormous sort of vault of inspiration” for her. At the link in bio, the actor reflects with Vogue on the end of "Euphoria," and how she's preparing for her busiest year yet. Photos: @carlijnjacobs

posts.caption: Director Alice Winocour spent a year and a half embedding herself backstage at shows during Paris Fashion Week before writing her script for “Couture.” In doing so, she was able to observe the women behind the scenes who are rarely the subjects of attention themselves. That instinct—to turn the camera away from the runway and toward the workroom—is the subversive heart of the film, in theaters today. The movie follows a bit of a meta narrative: Angelina Jolie plays Maxine Walker, an American director making a fashion film who receives a breast cancer diagnosis mid-shoot. The film effectively takes its structure from the craft it canonizes, creating a portrait of an artist through the women with whom she interacts. It also happens to be the first narrative film ever shot inside Chanel’s haute couture atelier, and that access—not to mention the permission to erase every logo—grounds the film, unexpectedly, in a communal act of creation. Get all the details at the link in our bio.

Not all peptides are created equal. From sagging skin to impaired skin barriers, peptide skin-care products have the unique ability to target a host of skin concerns in part because there are so many different types of peptides. At the link in our bio, discover everything you need to know about the most effective peptides for skin directly from dermatologists.

posts.alt_text: @MillieBobbyBrown's demi updo is the perfect summer style for long hair. On the press circuit for “Enola Holmes,” the star wore her hair in a half-up, half-down style with a deep side part and a bun positioned on the crown of her head. Created by her go-to hairstylist @PeteBurkill, tap the link in our bio to see more of MBB's recent beauty moments and discover why this 'do is perfect for the sunny days ahead.

posts.caption: Beach, please! No city escape is complete without a good book. We don’t begrudge the Kindle users—but there’s nothing quite like cracking the spine of a new novel, the lapping waves offering the purest white-noise soundtrack there is. But how to get your beach read to your destination? We have some suggestions—classy bags for classy books, some of the summer’s best. Tap the link in our bio for Vogue's list of this summer's best reads. Photographed by @matinzad, Prop styling by @cdaryla

posts.caption: "Saltburn," Whit Stillman’s "Metropolitan," "Brideshead Revisited." What you see in Jonathan Anderson’s @Dior Men will depend on your age and your media diet. Anderson addressed the multigenerational potential of the collection after the show: “There are things in here that my dad could wear, that I could wear,” he told @SarahMower_. “But fashion as a totemic look? I don’t know about that. I kind of enjoy screwing with it. Fashion has to be enjoyable. It has to be fun.” See every look at the link in bio. Backstage photographs by @styledumonde
posts.alt_text: For his spring 2027 collection, designer Willy Chavarria took his signature silhouettes — inspired by his Chicano heritage — and lightened them up. In addition to his usual rich color play, he introduced florals and played with a dressed-undressed dichotomy. Oh, and yes, that was Knicks point guard Jordan Clarkson on the runway. Discover the collection at the link in bio.

posts.caption: Before @KidSuper was on the runway, Colm Dillane was on the field. Tonight in Miami, with the World Cup top of mind, the designer brought that lifelong love of soccer full circle, collaborating with more than 50 artists from around the world on his spring 2027 collection.

posts.caption: Today, @rickowensonline once again delivered a show of fashion foresight. This collection applied Owens’s signature dark harmony to tension. Its clearest structural metaphor came in the tensegrity chaps, hand-built by Straytukay in foam and latex, resembling insectoid exoskeletons. Pre-show, Owens explained the concept was inspired by Buckminster Fuller’s term for systems in which isolated components under compression are held within networks of continuous tension. He connected it to both body and mind: “We feel like an architecture of tensions: it holds us together.” That idea defined the collection. Training emerged as one coping strategy, expressed through his first Adidas collaboration since 2013–2017. New footwear included a loose gaiter-sock and a runner with an engorged sole, designed as an accessible, high-performance running shoe built with Adidas’s technical expertise. Photographs by @styledumonde, Videos by @luke_leitch

posts.caption:
Years of dedicated research and winemaking expertise has led to this. Rooted in the province of Treviso, Bottega SpA expresses the excellence of Italian savoir-faire. At the heart of its identity lies Prosecco: fresh, lively and versatile beyond measure. The platinum standard? The elegant Gold Diamond Prosecco. Discover what it takes to dream up the wondrous, sparkling wine, and how sustainability is integral to the company’s philosophy, now in the link in bio. #VogueSingapore #bottegamoments
#italiansparklinglife
#premiumvintagecollection
posts.alt_text:
Japanese artistry is taken to new heights in @suntorywhisky_hibiki newest campaign, starring the brand’s first-ever global ambassador @annasawai. The partnership between Sawai and Hibiki comes as no surprise: the country’s artisans—across various facets of craftsmanship—are renowned for honouring tradition while maintaining the highest standards of excellence.

In the link in bio, Sawai sits down with #VogueSingapore to discuss the common thread tying the art of acting with the mastery of Hibiki whisky, as well as the timeless power of single-minded craftsmanship, in her new role with the whisky label. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
"How do you create something today that you couldn't make yesterday?" asks #JackMcCollough. Sitting at the helm of @loewe with #LazaroHernandez may be a colossal task but the designer pair are taking it in their stride.
"For us, we craft the 21st centry. Tools are available to us now that allow us to make things we couldn't do last season or even yesterday." So for their sophomore collection at #Loewe, it was about pushing technology, developing what 'craft' means and learning new ideas of making.
It's a sign that only good things are to come as the duo embark on a new journey with the maison. "It's like a new book. We wrote a book and we closed it—it was a great book, but now it's on the shelf and we're working on a new kind of book. The road ahead is wide open."
For our 'Nomad' May issue, the two take #VogueSingapore through their journey to and at #Loewe, one filled with play, exploration and discovery.
Snag a copy of the issue, now in the link in bio.

posts.caption:
#JackMcCollough and #LazaroHernandez had plenty to prove when they took up the role as @loewe's creative directors last year. When asked about the sort of pressure they were under, McCollough shares: "In some ways it feels so different. A sophomore collection feels like the stakes are higher. I think expectations are higher with the second collection, so perhaps we felt more pressure on that level. But we are best under pressure. We rise to the occasion."
And rose they did—as the show proved a fresh slate for the craft aspect of the brand. From vintage-inspired colourful slip dresses of metal-moulded latex or silicone pieces that "feel almost like they're from the future" to the rediscovered, single-strap Amazona 180, the designer duo has certainly been giving the Spanish house some new points of view.
Lensed in Paris, the exuberant collection comes to life on the cover of the 'Nomad' May issue and inside the book. Read more about McCollough and Hernandez's journey and how they're just getting started at @loewe, now in the link in bio.
Snag the 'Nomad' May issue now, out on newsstands on 8 May.

posts.alt_text:
Japanese artistry is taken to new heights in @suntorywhisky_hibiki newest campaign, starring the brand’s first-ever global ambassador @annasawai. The partnership between Sawai and Hibiki comes as no surprise: the country’s artisans—across various facets of craftsmanship—are renowned for honouring tradition while maintaining the highest standards of excellence.

In the link in bio, Sawai sits down with #VogueSingapore to discuss the common thread tying the art of acting with the mastery of Hibiki whisky, as well as the timeless power of single-minded craftsmanship, in her new role with the whisky label. #VogueSingapore

posts.caption:
Fashion rarely stands still. In 2025, one of the most controversial seasons in recent memory unfolded, and amidst a wave of designer musical chairs, #JackMcCollough and #LazaroHernandez were announced at the helm of @loewe.
"It is part of a new era, a new story and a new chapter in fashion. To be part of that was quite a monumental shift in the industry. It was extraordinary and we are so grateful," Hernandez muses. A year into their tenure, the timing feels almost poetic: #Loewe turns 180.
For the cover of our 'Nomad' May issue, we lens their sophomore collection for the Spanish maison on the streets of Paris; where Bebe Parnell leans into its movement, unlocking the zany, plsyfu mood offered by the clothes. In the link in bio, the designer duo speaks to #VogueSingapore on redefining craft for a new era.
Snag the 'Nomad' May issue now, out on newsstands on 8 May.

posts.caption: What does it truly mean to live well? That’s the question we’re asking this June, at the inaugural #VogueWellnessDay. In line with Global Wellness Day, #VogueSingapore is hosting an engaging and immersive one-day event at @mettsingapore, opening up conversations regarding all facets of wellness. Taking place on 6 June, the full-day event will be anchored by a slate of meaningful panel discussions, masterclasses and immersive workshops—dedicated to topics of longevity, nutrition and even mental resilience. Swipe to discover the highlights of the first Vogue Wellness Day and hit the link in bio to read more.

posts.caption: As part of #MilanDesignWeek, @prada held its fifth instalment of #PradaFrames, the annual conference created to prompt healthy discourse and cross-disciplinary dialogue on culture, design and society at large. This year’s edition, titled ‘In Sight’, was focussed on the art of image-making—a topic that is only increasing in significance as the worlds of fashion and art walk the line between human-authored creativity and machine-generated material. Swipe to see highlights from the symposium, held as part of this year’s #SalonedelMobile. #VogueSingapore

posts.alt_text:
A stage. A story. A Rajkumari. 

RAJKUMARI is back, MY BIGGEST INDIA TOUR YET. (If you don’t see your city don’t worry, more cities to be added). 
Tickets on Book My Show.

What is Rajkumari?

An evening of stories, poems dipped in music. This is not just a show. It’s a gentle rebellion. Against everything you were told to be.A space to see differently.
To live differently. Your way. A reminder of the power of your own perspective, of your “Nayab Nazariya”.

After touring in 6 countries, Rajkumari is back in India. Come for one of India’s biggest storytelling & Poetry show.

Tickets on @bookmyshowin , link in my bio🔗

posts.caption:
✨ Creator, Actor, PHENOMENON! ✨ On the June-July cover of Wedding Vows, we present the trailblazing @mostlysane, Prajakta Koli!
From building a YouTube empire to being the lead for Netflix Original Mismatched, from being part of Michelle Obama’s Day Time Emmy winning docu-series to being UNDP India's First Youth Climate Champion, her journey is nothing short of extraordinary.
With her debut romance novel set to launch this year, along with her first Amazon Prime Original Andhera, Prajakta’s journey is a testament to the power of dreams, hard work, and staying true to oneself. She continues to inspire millions with her authenticity, relatability, and unstoppable spirit.

posts.caption:
Some stories don’t need a synopsis. They need a setting.
Raja @designby_raja has been building those settings his entire career. Flagship weddings across India and beyond. Spaces that make people stop and forget to breathe. Hyderabad’s finest, and the wedding world knows it.
When we said we’re bringing WV Connect to Hyderabad, he didn’t ask questions. He opened doors. Became our Strategic Alliance Partner and made sure this city was ready to hold something this big.
In our boardroom, Raja was the one who understood instinctively what this edition needed to feel like. Because he’s spent his whole life making celebrations feel like cinema before cinema was even part of the conversation.
Lights. Camera. Shaadi. He was ready before we even said it. 🎬🧡
WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad
Venue Partner: @arthresorts
An Experience by @weddingvows.in
In Strategic Alliance with @designby_raja
#LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana
posts.alt_text:
Every great film has that one person on set who holds everything together without making it about themselves.

Rituraj sir @_rituraj333 is that person for WV Connect.

As CEO of Q Events by Geeta Samuel @qeventsbygeetasamuel , he has spent decades turning wedding days into legacies. Destination spectacles. Intimate masterpieces. Work that the industry still references when it talks about what excellence looks like.

Four seasons of WV Connect. Four seasons of him in the boardroom, not just as President but as the person who made every voice in that room feel worth hearing. That’s a rare thing. Rarer than any title.

When we brought the Weddings & Films dream to the table, he didn’t just nod. He leaned in.

The Godfather is coming to Hyderabad. And the city is ready. 🎬🧡

WV Connect Season 4 | Sep 1, 2 & 3, 2026 | Arth Resorts & Spa, Hyderabad

Venue Partner : @arthresorts
An Experience by @weddingvows.in
In Strategic Alliance with @designby_raja

#LightsCameraShaadi #WVConnect2026 #HyderabadZaroorAana

posts.caption:
Rei Kawakubo’s spring show for @commedesgarcons Homme Plus felt like a bookend to her nearly heartbreaking fall 2025 men’s effort, titled “To Hell With War.”⁠
⁠
Here it was “If the War Were to End,” and the spectacle brimmed with hope, vivid color and positive vibes.⁠
⁠
A year-and-a-half ago, her young models wore army boots with the toe box upturned at a 90-degree angle, a footwear style that suggested war is a dead end.⁠
⁠
Here, the boots had morphed into poulaines, their long, pointy toes curled up whimsically. Likewise, camouflage prints were blown up in size and recolored in psychedelic hues, bringing lava lamps to mind. Army helmets had been replaced by calico sun hats or bucket styles.⁠
⁠
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio⁠
⁠
Report: Miles Socha⁠
Photos: @giovanni_giannoni_photo/WWD
posts.alt_text:
The stars stayed cool in the summer heat with cold pints of beer and ice on their wrists at @jessicamccormack’s Summer Solstice Party this week at Mount Street Gardens in Mayfair.

Diamonds weren’t the only things shining on the night. Cohosts Zoë Kravitz and Carey Mulligan cohosted “McCormack’s Tavern,” a British pub-inspired garden party with guests including Drew Barrymore, Jodie Comer, Caitriona Balfe, Alexa Chung, Poppy Delevingne and Sheila Atim.

Kravitz, a brand ambassador who describes McCormack’s rocks as “diamonds with attitude,” wore a thick, white gold armband emblazoned with “JMc,” and embellished with a seven-carat diamond charm. She paired the sparkles with a sleek one-shouldered gray slipdress.

Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio

Report/Video: @mary.wenthur

posts.caption:
While Sarah Burton debuted her first @Givenchy presentation during men’s fashion week in Paris on Thursday, it’s hardly her first effort.
She’s been quietly overseeing men’s collections since being named creative director of the heritage house in late 2024, and menswear tailoring, one of her passions, has already informed her womenswear collections.
“The two collections talk to each other,” she said during a preview Tuesday evening, where looks from her spring 2027 menswear collection were displayed on mannequins amid sculptures by acclaimed English artist Rachel Whiteread.
“When I arrived, I put the design studios next to each other, so that the women’s and men’s teams talk to each other,” she said. “It’s almost a constant conversation that happens between the two studios, and sometimes an idea originates in men’s and then comes over to women’s and vice versa.”
Read more at WWD.com — #linkinbio
Report: @milessocha

posts.caption:
Grace @WalesBonner described her collection as a “study in refinement and purity,” as she took inspiration from studio portraiture as well as documentary photography. She wanted to look at different characters and modes of self-expression, which is why there were some lovely contrasts, and quirky details.
Fabrics ranged from washed velvet, cotton and linen with a worn-in feel to a tougher python-printed leather. A stone-colored chore jacket was soft and slouchy, and came paired with matching workwear trousers with loops for tools and little rivets.
Knits ranged from the ribbed, striped and sporty to the grandmotherly, as in a taupe twin set with burgundy rosettes embroidered around the collar. Polo styles had a polished 1950s feel, and came in punchy color combinations — cobalt and black, cream and burgundy, which Wales Bonner teamed with drawstring shorts.
Read the full collection review at WWD.com - #linkinbio
Report: @samanthavconti

posts.caption:
@camimendes is having one of the biggest years in her career so far. A major summer blockbuster, the second feature film from her production company, and wedding planning are all on the roster for 2026. This month, Mendes steps into the sprawling world of Masters of the Universe as the sword-slinging Teela alongside Nicholas Galitzine, Idris Elba, and Jared Leto.
The project is a stretch from her days as Veronica Lodge in #Riverdale, which catapulted her into mainstream recognition nearly a decade ago. Mendes makes movies for “the girls and the gays,” she states lovingly, but her star power is only going to get bigger and brighter with Masters. “I’ve never been a part of something of this scale,” she says of the project.
In our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, we chat with the actress about reinventing herself in this era of her life. Read it all in the full interview at the link in our bio.

posts.alt_text:
There would be no Pope Heyward without @jonathandavissofficial. Much like his smart, careful, deeply moral Outer Banks character, Daviss knows what it feels like to carry the pressure of proving your worth and refusing to let yourself or anyone else down. “What really stood out to me about Pope in season 1 was this idea of expectations and pressure that gets put on a lot of young Black teens and teens in general,” he says. “There’s this pressure to succeed, and it was something I truly have been through.”

Daviss still thinks back to his early audition days before landing the life-changing role on OBX, when the constant need to prove himself as a young actor felt emotionally exhausting. His mindset then? “You only lose when you quit.”

Head to the link in our bio for the full download in our Now Playing Issue presented by @bvlgari, from what to expect from the fifth and final season of OBX to his upcoming portrayal of a young Snoop Dogg.

posts.caption:
@victoriamonet is trading her claws for butterfly wings. The three-time Grammy winner is the definition of a multi-hyphenate: a culinary school graduate, a mother, and, of course, a singer-songwriter with a nearly two-decade-spanning catalog to her name. Now, using her 37th birthday as a launching pad, Monét has cleared her mental space and scrubbed her socials to prepare for her next chapter—an album she’s dubbed her very own Thriller, a sonically surprising, genre-defying project that marks a softer evolution. “The jaguar exists within me always, but I’m feeling like a softer version of myself now,” she says.
After all, Monét’s critically acclaimed debut studio album Jaguar II was the ultimate learning lesson. “Once you have an accolade like that, people often look for a reason to discredit why you got it,” she explains. “I think—pending a Grammy happens again—I would do the opposite and bunker down in the studio.”
Step inside Monét’s world in our Now Playing Issue, presented by @bvlgari, at the link in our bio.
